title: Dynamic Treatment Regime Learning with DRMLSurv
text: DRMLSurv R package dynamic treatment regime learning two-stage survival settings censoring. package designed analyses treatment decisions made sequentially, event times censored, inferential target treatment rule rather single static treatment contrast. many clinical applications, subject receives initial treatment stage 1, may may proceed stage 2, overall survival outcome may incompletely observed. settings, valid regime learning requires direct comparison observed outcomes. One must account censoring, construct appropriate stage-specific overall counterfactual outcomes, estimate treatment rules using completed matched pseudo-outcomes. DRMLSurv workflow combines four main components: nuisance-score estimation, including treatment, prognostic, censoring-related scores; matching-based imputation censored stage-specific outcomes; construction counterfactual regime-specific outcomes; policy learning using random forests related machine-learning procedures. vignette gives single end--end overview package workflow explains main functions fit together. code examples intentionally schematic written clarify workflow. practice, exact variable names, covariate sets, matching restrictions, learner libraries, tuning grids reflect scientific problem structure available data.

title: Dynamic Treatment Regime Learning with DRMLSurv
text: Consider two-stage treatment setting. Let A1∈{−1,1}A_1 \\{-1,1\} denote stage-1 treatment, A2∈{−1,1}A_2 \\{-1,1\} denote stage-2 treatment among enter stage 2, η2∈{0,1}\eta_2 \\{0,1\} denote stage-2 entry, Y1Y_1 denote stage-1 survival time, Y2Y_2 denote stage-2 survival time, YY denote overall survival time, Δ∈{0,1}\Delta \\{0,1\} denote event indicator. target dynamic treatment regime d=(d1,d2), d = (d_1, d_2),  d1(𝐱)d_1(\mathbf{x}) assigns stage-1 treatment based baseline covariates d2(𝐱)d_2(\mathbf{x}) assigns stage-2 treatment based relevant second-stage information. general statistical objective identify treatment rule d⋆d^\star optimizes policy value V(d)=𝔼{Yd}, V(d) = \mathop{\mathrm{\mathbb{E}}}\{Y^{d}\},  YdY^d counterfactual overall survival outcome regime dd. YdY^d directly observed treatment paths, package builds completed regime-specific outcomes matching imputation.

title: Dynamic Treatment Regime Learning with DRMLSurv
text: typical dataset DRMLSurv contains: individual identifier; stage-2 entry indicator; stage-1 stage-2 treatment variables; stage-specific overall survival times; censoring/event indicator; stage-specific covariates. common variable layout : patientid: subject identifier; eta2: stage-2 entry indicator; deathInd.raw: event indicator; OS_time: observed overall survival time; OS_time.1L: stage-1 survival time; OS_time.2L: stage-2 survival time; txgroup1L.sd: stage-1 treatment; txgroup2L0.sd: stage-2 treatment. package dataset, load first. may verify required variables present.
code: data("DATASET", package = "DRMLSurv") dat <- DATASET required_vars <- c(   "patientid", "eta2", "deathInd.raw",   "OS_time", "OS_time.1L", "OS_time.2L",   "txgroup1L.sd", "txgroup2L0.sd" )  setdiff(required_vars, names(dat))

title: Dynamic Treatment Regime Learning with DRMLSurv
text: package includes rfdtr() random-forest-based policy learning. function uses matched labels, matched outcomes, optional hyperparameter tuning learn treatment rules. stage-2 analysis, one may form learning dataset : returned object contains fitted model, estimated optimal treatments observed sample, tuning summary, selected best tuning parameters.
code: obs_stage2 <- data.frame(   A = dat_2L$newTxt,   dat_2L[, c("ageAt1L.sd", "gender.sd", "Albumin2nd.sd", "Lymphocyte2nd.sd"), drop = FALSE] )  gridpar <- expand.grid(   mtry     = c(1, 2, 3),   ntree    = c(500),   nodesize = c(2, 5) )  rf_fit <- rfdtr(   modeltype = "ranger",   usecv     = TRUE,   sl.seed   = 123,   obs       = obs_stage2,   W         = dat_2L$match.weight,   gridpar   = gridpar,   metric    = "policyval",   A.obs     = dat_2L$txgroup2L0.sd,   Q.obs     = dat_2L$compOS2L,   Q.match   = dat_2L$pairedCompOS2L )  rf_fit$best

title: Two-stage DTR learning/evaluation pipeline with cross-fitting — Drmatch
text: simrun() runs one replicate (indexed jj) two-stage dynamic treatment regime (DTR) learning evaluation pipeline using outer cross-fitting (default 5 folds). Within fold, function imputes censoring-related composite outcomes training partition, constructs double scores (propensity/prognostic scores) requested, performs matching-based pseudo-outcome construction stage 2 stage 1, fits random-forest policies via rfdtr, applies learned rules held-fold, returns fold-level value summaries benchmark comparisons.

Columns include: Fold-level value summaries (means medians) learned DTR several alternative constructions produced inside pipeline (e.g., Ttot, T2, T2.rf, TtotOSml, Ttot1LOSmed, etc.). Agreement metrics: CCR1 (stage-1 agreement) CCR2 (stage-2 agreement among eta2==1). Observed uniform-regime benchmarks (means/medians) stage 2 overall. Diagnostics stored list-columns, including RF best hyperparameters predicted optimal actions. returned object also includes iter (replicate index) fold. fatal error occurs, NULL returned.

title: Two-stage DTR learning/evaluation pipeline with cross-fitting — Drmatch
text: outer loop 5-fold split created caret::createFolds() stage-1 observed treatment A1.var. fold: Split training test partitions. Standardize selected time variables training (OY.sd, Y1.sd, Y2.sd). Impute censoring-related composite outcomes training fold via impute_censored_outcomes (internally calling stage-2 stage-1 imputation). Optionally compute double scores imputed training fold via get_doublescores. Stage 2 (among eta2==1): build matched opposite-arm pseudo-outcomes via matchpotential_DTR, tune/fit random-forest policy via rfdtr, compute estimated stage-2 optimal value substituting matched outcomes units assigned non-optimal observed treatment. Stage 1: construct pseudo-outcome Rtilde (including estimated stage-2 optimal value stage-2 entrants), perform matching via matchpotential_DTR, tune/fit stage-1 policy via rfdtr. Apply learned policies held-fold compute fold-level summaries: value estimates (means medians, capped cap_months), agreement measures (CCR1, CCR2), observed/uniform benchmark regimes.

title: Summarize policy performance metrics for a two-stage treatment regime — policy_summary_metrics
text: Stage-1 stage-2 summaries include accuracy, Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC), sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value (PPV), negative predictive value (NPV), F1 score. joint two-stage treatment path, function additionally returns multiclass MCC together macro-averaged prevalence-weighted classification summaries across four possible treatment paths. Optional RMST-style value summaries can also computed corresponding outcome columns supplied. function assumes binary treatment coding values -1 1. Internally, estimated observed treatments converted factors levels c(-1, 1). Stage-1 metrics computed using complete cases estimated observed stage-1 treatment assignments. Stage-2 joint metrics computed among subjects satisfying eta2 == 1. Joint treatment paths defined four-level factor c("-1_-1", "-1_1", "1_-1", "1_1"), corresponding sequence stage-1 stage-2 treatment assignments. Macro-averaged metrics calculated unweighted mean class-wise statistics joint confusion matrix. Weighted summaries use empirical class prevalences observed joint treatment paths. Optional RMST-style summaries computed corresponding column names supplied found tmpData. use internal scoring rule assigns subject-level values based estimated treatment.

what: Tune and fit a random-forest DTR policy model with (optional) cross-validation — rfdtr
title: Tune and fit a random-forest DTR policy model with (optional) cross-validation — rfdtr
text: Fits classification-based dynamic treatment regime (DTR) policy model binary treatment learns binary decision rule \(\hat d(X)\) values \(\{-1,+1\}\) using either ranger randomForestSRC. function performs grid search random-forest tuning parameters , usecv=TRUE, evaluates candidate policies via K-fold cross-validation using: CCR: classification correctness rate (accuracy predicted treatment labels), OOB: --bag prediction error (model-reported), Score: user-supplied policy-value-like performance measure computed my_score.Surv() using observed matched pseudo-outcomes. best tuning parameters selected according metric, final model fit full observed dataset obs. output includes final fitted model, estimated treatment rule estA.obs, full tuning results.

title: Tune and fit a random-forest DTR policy model with (optional) cross-validation — rfdtr
code: rfdtr(   modeltype = "ranger",   usecv = TRUE,   sl.seed = 123,   obs,   W,   gridpar,   metric = "ccr",   A.obs,   Q.obs,   Q.match,   score_agg = c("mean", "sum") )

title: Tune and fit a random-forest DTR policy model with (optional) cross-validation — rfdtr
text: modeltype Character. Random-forest engine: "ranger" "rfsrc". usecv Logical. TRUE, uses 5-fold CV tune hyperparameters; otherwise tunes full data. sl.seed Integer. Seed reproducibility. (Note: parts function currently hard-code set.seed(123).) obs data.frame. Training dataset containing column covariates predicting . W Numeric vector. Case weights length nrow(obs). gridpar data.frame. Grid tuning parameters. Expected columns include: ntree, mtry, nodesize. Extra columns ignored model fits. metric Character. Criterion used choose “best” tuning row. Supported: "oob": minimize OOB "policyval" / "score" / "policy" / "val": maximize Score otherwise: maximize CCR .obs Numeric vector. Observed treatment labels (-1/1) aligned obs. Q.obs Numeric vector. Observed pseudo-outcome value component aligned obs. Q.match Numeric vector. Matched/pair pseudo-outcome aligned obs used my_score.Surv(). score_agg Character. Aggregation fold-level Score: "sum" "mean".

title: Tune and fit a random-forest DTR policy model with (optional) cross-validation — rfdtr
text: list elements: model: final fitted ranger randomForestSRC object trained obs. estA.obs: numeric vector predicted treatment decisions (-1/1) row obs. tune: data.frame tuning results row gridpar, including CCR, OOB, Score. best: selected row tune corresponding chosen metric.

title: Tune and fit a random-forest DTR policy model with (optional) cross-validation — rfdtr
text: Inputs data layout. obs data.frame must contain column named covariates used model . function coerces obs$factor levels c(-1, 1) ensure binary classification target. W numeric vector case weights aligned obs (e.g., IPC weights). .obs numeric observed treatment indicator (typically -1/1) aligned obs. Q.obs Q.match numeric vectors aligned obs used my_score.Surv() compute policy score (e.g., observed pseudo-outcome matched/pair pseudo-outcome). Cross-validation logic. usecv=TRUE, function uses 5-fold stratified folds created caret::createFolds(obs$, k=5). candidate parameter set gridpar, fits model training folds evaluates held-fold: CCR: mean(predicted_class == obs$[test]). OOB: model-reported OOB error (ranger, mod$prediction.error; rfsrc, last entry mod$err.rate[,""]). Score: my_score.Surv(pred, .test, Q.test, Q.match.test) pred numeric treatment rule -1/1. Aggregation score across folds. per-fold Score values aggregated across CV folds using: score_agg="sum": sum fold scores (ignoring NA) score_agg="mean": mean fold scores (ignoring NA) controls scale used model selection metric targets policy value. Parameter guards. avoid invalid random-forest hyperparameters, gridpar includes mtry, clamped [1, p] p = ncol(obs)-1 (.e., columns except ). Duplicate parameter rows removed via unique(gridpar). Parallelization. function written using foreach %dopar%. assumes parallel backend already registered (e.g., via doParallel). Within fold, num.threads=1 used ranger avoid nested parallelism.
